books.title: Antic Hay
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.summary: A group of 1920s London socialites struggle with their futures.

books.title: Lorna Doone
books.author: R. D. Blackmore
books.summary: A young yeoman farmer falls in love with a beautiful girl who belongs to the outlaw clan which killed his father.

books.title: The Diary
books.author: Samuel Pepys
books.summary: The diary of a man living in London in the 17th century.

books.title: Personal Recollections of Joan of Arc
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A fictional biography of Joan of Arc from her early teenage years to her martyrdom.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A collection of George MacDonald’s fairy tales, short stories, and novellas.

books.title: Jack Keefe Stories
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A boastful, nearly illiterate major league pitcher writes a series of letters home detailing the goings-on in his life and career.

books.title: The Trachiniae
books.author: Sophocles
books.summary: A woman unintentionally kills her demigod husband with a poisoned robe in a desperate attempt to reclaim his love.

books.title: The Lost Continent
books.author: C. J. Cutcliffe Hyne
books.summary: A swashbuckling adventure on the fabled continent of Atlantis.

books.title: Lord Tony’s Wife
books.author: Baroness Orczy
books.summary: A young woman and her father are kidnapped by a man intent on revenge for his father’s death.

books.title: The Three Impostors
books.author: Arthur Machen
books.summary: Two flâneurs in Victorian London get caught up with the members of a secret society and their search for a rare Roman coin.

books.title: The Mirror of the Sea
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: An autobiographical memoir on Joseph Conrad’s maritime experiences and his relationship with the sea.

books.title: The Red Thumbmark
books.author: R. Austin Freeman
books.summary: A detective struggles to prove the innocence of a man accused of stealing jewels, in the face of overwhelming evidence.

books.title: The Federalist Papers
books.author: Alexander Hamilton
books.summary: Eighty-five articles written by a group of U.S. Founding Fathers on why the proposed U.S. Constitution should be approved.

books.title: Democracy and Social Ethics
books.author: Jane Addams
books.summary: Jane Addams argues that addressing social issues like poverty and inequality requires shifting from individual morality to a collective understanding of social responsibility.

books.title: Poems on Various Subjects, Religious and Moral
books.author: Phillis Wheatley
books.summary: Phillis Wheatley’s only published collection of poetry.

books.title: The Indiscreet Jewels
books.author: Denis Diderot
books.summary: The Sultan of the Congo brings about philosophical inquiry and scandal when he is granted a magical ring that permits him to compel speech from the vaginas of the women of his court.

books.title: The War of the Worlds
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: The first-person narrative of the Martian invasion of 19th-century Earth.

books.title: School Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of Wodehouse’s school stories about the lives and adventures of schoolboys in the early 1900s.

books.title: The City of God
books.author: Augustine of Hippo
books.summary: Bishop Augustine of Hippo provides a defense of the Christian faith against its pagan critics, as well as explaining God’s work and purposes in world history, from beginning to end.

books.title: With the Empress Dowager of China
books.author: Katharine A. Carl
books.summary: A female painter becomes the court artist of the Empress Dowager of China.
